name: gcp
context: Terraform Google Cloud provider — configure, authenticate, and manage GCP resources including Compute Engine, GCS, Cloud SQL, GKE, and Cloud Run
info: The Google Cloud Platform provider for Terraform (hashicorp/google) enables declarative management of GCP resources through HCL. It covers Compute Engine instances, Cloud Storage buckets, Cloud SQL databases, GKE clusters, Cloud Run services, IAM bindings, VPC networks, and many more GCP services. Authentication follows GCP's Application Default Credentials (ADC) chain: GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS environment variable, gcloud auth application-default login, service account key files, workload identity federation, and compute engine metadata. The provider requires a project ID and region, which can be set in the provider block or via environment variables. The google-beta provider gives access to GCP features in beta before they reach general availability.
triggers: deploy to google cloud with terraform
set up workload identity for terraform

sections.title: Provider configuration
sections.body: Declare the Google provider in `required_providers` with `source = "hashicorp/google"` and a version constraint (e.g., `~> 5.0`). The `provider "google"` block accepts `project` (GCP project ID), `region`, `zone`, and `credentials`. Project, region, and zone can also be set via `GOOGLE_PROJECT`, `GOOGLE_REGION`, and `GOOGLE_ZONE` environment variables. Resources inherit these defaults but can override them. For beta GCP features, add the `hashicorp/google-beta` provider and set `provider = google-beta` on resources that need it.

sections.title: Authentication and credentials
sections.body: The Google provider uses Application Default Credentials (ADC): (1) `GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS` env var pointing to a service account key JSON, (2) `gcloud auth application-default login` for local development, (3) explicit `credentials` argument in the provider block, (4) Workload Identity Federation for CI/CD (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI), (5) Compute Engine metadata for code running on GCE/GKE/Cloud Run. For production CI/CD, use Workload Identity Federation to avoid service account key files. The `GOOGLE_IMPERSONATE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT` env var enables service account impersonation without a key file.

sections.title: Most-used GCP resources
sections.body: The most commonly managed GCP resources: `google_compute_instance` (VMs), `google_storage_bucket` (object storage), `google_sql_database_instance` (Cloud SQL), `google_container_cluster` and `google_container_node_pool` (GKE Kubernetes), `google_cloud_run_service` (serverless containers), `google_project_iam_member` (IAM bindings), `google_compute_network` and `google_compute_subnetwork` (VPC), `google_compute_firewall` (firewall rules), `google_service_account` (service accounts), and `google_pubsub_topic` (messaging). Each resource maps to a GCP API resource with full CRUD lifecycle support.

sections.title: Most-used GCP data sources
sections.body: Data sources query existing GCP state: `data.google_project` returns project metadata including the numeric project number. `data.google_compute_network` looks up existing VPC networks by name. `data.google_client_config` provides the current access token, project, and region, which is essential for configuring the Kubernetes provider to authenticate with GKE. `data.google_compute_zones` lists available zones in a region. `data.google_compute_image` finds images by family for use in instance boot disks. `data.google_iam_policy` generates IAM policy documents.

scripts.name: Verify GCP Credentials
scripts.description: Verify Google Cloud credentials are configured, display the active project, account, and list available services
scripts.inline:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

echo '=== GCP Credential Verification ==='
echo ''

# Check if gcloud is available
if ! command -v gcloud &>/dev/null; then
  echo 'ERROR: gcloud CLI not found. Install it from https://cloud.google.com/sdk/install'
  exit 1
fi

# Display active configuration
echo '--- Active Configuration ---'
ACCOUNT=$(gcloud config get-value account 2>/dev/null || echo 'not set')
PROJECT=$(gcloud config get-value project 2>/dev/null || echo 'not set')
REGION=$(gcloud config get-value compute/region 2>/dev/null || echo 'not set')
ZONE=$(gcloud config get-value compute/zone 2>/dev/null || echo 'not set')
echo "  Account: $ACCOUNT"
echo "  Project: $PROJECT"
echo "  Region:  $REGION"
echo "  Zone:    $ZONE"

echo ''
echo '--- Application Default Credentials ---'
if [ -n "${GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS:-}" ]; then
  echo "  GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS: $GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS"
  if [ -f "$GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS" ]; then
    echo '  Key file exists: yes'
  else
    echo '  Key file exists: NO (file not found)'
  fi
else
  ADC_PATH="${HOME}/.config/gcloud/application_default_credentials.json"
  if [ -f "$ADC_PATH" ]; then
    echo '  Using gcloud application-default credentials'
  else
    echo '  No ADC found. Run: gcloud auth application-default login'
  fi
fi

echo ''
echo '--- Auth Test ---'
if gcloud auth print-access-token &>/dev/null; then
  echo '  Authentication: OK'
else
  echo '  Authentication: FAILED'
  echo '  Run: gcloud auth login'
  exit 1
fi

echo ''
echo '--- Terraform Check ---'
if command -v terraform &>/dev/null; then
  echo "  Terraform version: $(terraform version -json 2>/dev/null | python3 -c 'import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)["terraform_version"])' 2>/dev/null || terraform version | head -1)"
else
  echo '  Terraform not installed. Install from https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/install'
fi

echo ''
echo 'Credentials verified successfully.'

GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS.description: Path to service account key JSON file (optional if using gcloud auth or compute metadata)

GOOGLE_PROJECT.description: GCP project ID (optional if set in gcloud config)

credentials.description: Path to service account key JSON file

account.description: Active service account or user email

depends.reason: The GCP provider is a Terraform provider plugin
